title: dipsacus fullonum subsp. laciniatus
intro: Dipsacus fullonum subsp. laciniatus, also known as laciniated teasel, is a plant species in the teasel family that is characterized by its distinctive, deeply cut leaves and tall, upright growth habit. Traditionally, the plant has been used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic effects, due to the presence of sesquiterpene lactones and other bioactive compounds. The health benefits of laciniated teasel include potential applications in wound healing, pain relief, and the treatment of various gastrointestinal issues. Laciniated teasel is typically prepared as a tea, tincture, or infusion, with the dried flowers and leaves being used to make a herbal remedy for various purposes.
